reforms.description: The Finnish government is implementing a comprehensive reform package aimed at addressing skills shortages and enhancing access to tertiary education. This includes increasing the tertiary attainment rate for the 25-34 age group to 50% by 2030, increasing study places in higher education institutions by nearly 6,000 during 2021-2022, and formulating an employment package to generate 31,000–36,000 jobs by 2029, particularly focusing on older workers and unskilled adults.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing access to education and increasing job opportunities, the reform is expected to improve the skill level of the workforce, thereby raising productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced by the government.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in the education and employment landscape of Finland.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to implement reforms to ease skills shortages. The residence permit process for specialists will be streamlined to attract more foreign skilled workers.
